-- $Id: testes/closure.lua $
-- See Copyright Notice in file all.lua

print "testing closures"

local A,B = 0,{g=10}
local function f(x)
  local a = {}
  for i=1,1000 do
    local y = 0
    do
      a[i] = function () B.g = B.g+1; y = y+x; return y+A end
    end
  end
  local dummy = function () return a[A] end
  collectgarbage()
  A = 1; assert(dummy() == a[1]); A = 0;
  assert(a[1]() == x)
  assert(a[3]() == x)
  collectgarbage()
  assert(B.g == 12)
  return a
end

local a = f(10)
-- force a GC in this level
local x = {[1] = {}}   -- to detect a GC
setmetatable(x, {__mode = 'kv'})
while x[1] do   -- repeat until GC
  local a = A..A..A..A  -- create garbage
  A = A+1
end
assert(a[1]() == 20+A)
assert(a[1]() == 30+A)
assert(a[2]() == 10+A)
collectgarbage()
assert(a[2]() == 20+A)
assert(a[2]() == 30+A)
assert(a[3]() == 20+A)
assert(a[8]() == 10+A)
assert(getmetatable(x).__mode == 'kv')
assert(B.g == 19)


-- testing equality
a = {}

for i = 1, 5 do  a[i] = function (x) return i + a + _ENV end  end
assert(a[3] ~= a[4] and a[4] ~= a[5])

do
  local a = function (x)  return math.sin(_ENV[x])  end
  local function f()
    return a
  end
  assert(f() == f())
end


-- testing closures with 'for' control variable
a = {}
for i=1,10 do
  a[i] = {set = function(x) i=x end, get = function () return i end}
  if i == 3 then break end
end
assert(a[4] == undef)
a[1].set(10)
assert(a[2].get() == 2)
a[2].set('a')
assert(a[3].get() == 3)
assert(a[2].get() == 'a')

a = {}
local t = {"a", "b"}
for i = 1, #t do
  local k = t[i]
  a[i] = {set = function(x, y) i=x; k=y end,
          get = function () return i, k end}
  if i == 2 then break end
end
a[1].set(10, 20)
local r,s = a[2].get()
assert(r == 2 and s == 'b')
r,s = a[1].get()
assert(r == 10 and s == 20)
a[2].set('a', 'b')
r,s = a[2].get()
assert(r == "a" and s == "b")


-- testing closures with 'for' control variable x break
local f
for i=1,3 do
  f = function () return i end
  break
end
assert(f() == 1)

for k = 1, #t do
  local v = t[k]

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# run_official_test.sh <test_file>
#
# Wraps a single Lua 5.4 official test file (reference/lua-c/testes/*.lua)
# with a small preamble that defines the globals those tests assume the
# launcher set up (e.g. _soft, _port, _nomsg, arg) and feeds the whole
# thing into the lua-rs CLI. Captures stdout+stderr, prints a one-line
# PASS/FAIL summary, and (on failure) the first 5 lines of failure
# context.
#
# Usage:
#   ./harness/run_official_test.sh reference/lua-c/testes/vararg.lua

set -uo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)"
cd "$ROOT"

TEST_TIMEOUT_S=${TEST_TIMEOUT_S:-30}
BIN="$ROOT/target/debug/lua-rs"
OUT_DIR="harness/impl/official"
mkdir -p "$OUT_DIR"

if [ $# -lt 1 ]; then
    echo "usage: $0 <test_file>" >&2
    exit 2
fi

TEST_FILE="$1"
if [ ! -f "$TEST_FILE" ]; then
    echo "[err] test file not found: $TEST_FILE" >&2
    exit 2
fi

if [ ! -x "$BIN" ]; then
    echo "[err] lua-rs binary not built at $BIN" >&2
    exit 2
fi

BASE="$(basename "$TEST_FILE" .lua)"
COMBINED="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.combined.lua"
OUTFILE="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.out"

PREAMBLE='-- harness preamble: emulate the globals lua-c testes/all.lua sets
_soft = true
_port = true
_nomsg = true
_U = false
arg = arg or {}
_G = _G or _ENV
if _VERSION == nil then _VERSION = "Lua 5.4" end
'

{ printf '%s\n' "$PREAMBLE"; cat "$TEST_FILE"; } > "$COMBINED"

TESTES_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$TEST_FILE")" && pwd)"
export LUA_PATH="$TESTES_DIR/?.lua;$TESTES_DIR/?/init.lua;./?.lua;./?/init.lua"

run_with_timeout() {
    local src_file="$1"
    local out_file="$2"
    local src
    src="$(cat "$src_file")"
    if command -v gtimeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        gtimeout --signal=KILL "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1
    elif command -v timeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        timeout --signal=KILL "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1
    else
        ( "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1 ) &
        local pid=$!
        ( sleep "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" && kill -9 "$pid" 2>/dev/null ) &>/dev/null &
        local watcher=$!
        wait "$pid" 2>/dev/null
        local rc=$?
        kill "$watcher" &>/dev/null
        wait "$watcher" 2>/dev/null || true
        return $rc
    fi
}

run_with_timeout "$COMBINED" "$OUTFILE" || true

reached_exec=0
grep -qE "^\[4/4\] Executing chunk" "$OUTFILE" && reached_exec=1

if grep -qE "^\[ok\] execution completed" "$OUTFILE" \
    && ! grep -qE "not yet implemented:|^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at |^\[err\]" "$OUTFILE"; then
    printf "PASS  %s\n" "$TEST_FILE"
    exit 0
fi

if grep -qE "not yet implemented:" "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="stub"
elif grep -qE "panicked at .*parser" "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="parse-panic"
elif grep -qE "panicked at .*codegen|panicked at .*compil" "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="codegen-panic"
elif grep -qE "^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at " "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="runtime-panic"
elif grep -qE "^\[err\]" "$OUTFILE"; then
    if [ "$reached_exec" = "1" ]; then
        cause="runtime-error"
    else
        cause="parse-or-compile-error"
    fi
else
    cause="unknown"
fi

printf "FAIL  %s  (cause=%s, reached_exec=%s)\n" "$TEST_FILE" "$cause" "$reached_exec"
echo "--- first 5 lines of failure context ---"
{
    grep -nE "not yet implemented:|^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at |^\[err\]|panicked at " "$OUTFILE" \
        || tail -n 20 "$OUTFILE"
} | head -n 5
echo "--- (full output at $OUTFILE) ---"
exit 1

-- force a GC in this level
local x = {[1] = {}}   -- to detect a GC
setmetatable(x, {__mode = 'kv'})
while x[1] do   -- repeat until GC
  local a = A..A..A..A  -- create garbage
  A = A+1
end
assert(a[1]() == 20+A)
assert(a[1]() == 30+A)
assert(a[2]() == 10+A)
collectgarbage()
assert(a[2]() == 20+A)
assert(a[2]() == 30+A)
assert(a[3]() == 20+A)
assert(a[8]() == 10+A)
assert(getmetatable(x).__mode == 'kv')
assert(B.g == 19)


-- testing equality
a = {}

for i = 1, 5 do  a[i] = function (x) return i + a + _ENV end  end
assert(a[3] ~= a[4] and a[4] ~= a[5])

do
  local a = function (x)  return math.sin(_ENV[x])  end
  local function f()
    return a
  end
  assert(f() == f())
end


-- testing closures with 'for' control variable
a = {}
for i=1,10 do
  a[i] = {set = function(x) i=x end, get = function () return i end}
  if i == 3 then break end
end
assert(a[4] == undef)
a[1].set(10)
assert(a[2].get() == 2)
a[2].set('a')
assert(a[3].get() == 3)
assert(a[2].get() == 'a')

a = {}
local t = {"a", "b"}
for i = 1, #t do
  local k = t[i]
  a[i] = {set = function(x, y) i=x; k=y end,
          get = function () return i, k end}
  if i == 2 then break end
end
a[1].set(10, 20)
local r,s = a[2].get()
assert(r == 2 and s == 'b')
r,s = a[1].get()
assert(r == 10 and s == 20)
a[2].set('a', 'b')
r,s = a[2].get()
assert(r == "a" and s == "b")

for i=1,3 do
  f = function () return i end
  break
end
assert(f() == 1)

for k = 1, #t do

  f = function () return k, v end
  break
end
assert(({f()})[1] == 1)

220	///
221	/// C: `struct Table` in `lobject.h`.  The full field mapping is in
222	/// `ANALYSES/types.tsv` under `Table → LuaTable`.
223	///
224	/// # Dummy hash part
225	///
226	/// When a table has no hash part, `self.node` is empty and
227	/// `self.lastfree` is `None`.  This replaces the C idiom of storing a
228	/// pointer to a shared static `dummynode_` and checking `lastfree == NULL`.
229	pub struct LuaTable {
230	    /// Bit-packed: lower bits = absent-metamethod flags; bit 7 = BIT_RAS.
231	    /// C: `lu_byte flags`.
232	    pub flags: TableFlags,
233	    /// `log2` of the hash-part node count.  `1u32 << lsizenode` = capacity.
234	    /// C: `lu_byte lsizenode`.
235	    pub lsizenode: u8,
236	    /// Array-part size hint or real size depending on `flags.is_real_asize()`.
237	    /// C: `unsigned int alimit`.
238	    pub alimit: u32,
239	    /// The array part; 0-based in Rust (Lua index `k` → `self.array[k-1]`).
240	    /// C: `TValue *array`.
241	    pub array: Vec<LuaValue>,
242	    /// The hash part; length is `1 << lsizenode` when not dummy, 0 when dummy.
243	    /// C: `Node *node`.
244	    pub node: Vec<TableNode>,
245	    /// Cursor into `self.node` for free-slot scanning (one-past the last tried
246	    /// position).  `None` when using the dummy hash part.
247	    /// C: `Node *lastfree` (NULL = dummy).
248	    pub lastfree: Option<usize>,
249	    /// Optional metatable.
250	    /// C: `struct Table *metatable`.
251	    pub metatable: Option<GcRef<LuaTable>>,
252	}
253	
254	impl LuaTable {
255	    // ── Predicate helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
256	
257	    /// `isdummy(t)` — true when the table has no allocated hash part.
258	    #[inline]
259	    pub fn is_dummy(&self) -> bool {
260	        self.lastfree.is_none()
261	    }
262	
263	    /// `sizenode(t)` — nominal hash-part capacity (`1 << lsizenode`).
264	    /// This is the modulo used for hash indexing, not the vec length.
265	    #[inline]
266	    fn sizenode(&self) -> u32 {
267	        1u32 << self.lsizenode
268	    }
269	
270	    /// `allocsizenode(t)` — 0 when dummy, else `1 << lsizenode`.
271	    #[inline]
272	    pub fn alloc_sizenode(&self) -> u32 {
273	        if self.is_dummy() { 0 } else { self.sizenode() }
274	    }
275	
276	    /// `isrealasize(t)` accessor; delegates to `TableFlags`.
277	    #[inline]
278	    fn is_real_asize(&self) -> bool { self.flags.is_real_asize() }
279	
280	    // ── ispow2 (C macro with 0-as-pow2 semantics) ─────────────────────────
281	
282	    /// `ispow2(x)` — C treats 0 as a power of two (`(x & (x-1)) == 0`).
283	    #[inline]
284	    fn is_pow2(x: u32) -> bool {
285	        x == 0 || x.is_power_of_two()
286	    }
287	
288	    // ── luaH_realasize ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
289	
290	    /// Returns the real size of the array part.
291	    ///
292	    /// When `flags.is_real_asize()`, this is just `alimit`.  Otherwise
293	    /// `alimit` is a hint and the real size is the next power of two above it.
294	    ///
295	    /// C: `unsigned int luaH_realasize (const Table *t)` (`LUAI_FUNC`).
296	    pub fn real_asize(&self) -> u32 {
297	        // C: if (limitequalsasize(t)) return t->alimit;
298	        if self.limit_equals_asize() {
299	            return self.alimit;
300	        }
301	        // Compute the smallest power of 2 not smaller than alimit.
302	        let mut size = self.alimit;
303	        size |= size >> 1;
304	        size |= size >> 2;
305	        size |= size >> 4;
306	        size |= size >> 8;
307	        size |= size >> 16;
308	        // For 64-bit unsigned int Lua would also do >> 32; but alimit is u32 here.
309	        size = size.wrapping_add(1);
310	        debug_assert!(
311	            Self::is_pow2(size) && size / 2 < self.alimit && self.alimit < size
312	        );
313	        size
314	    }
315	
316	    /// `limitequalsasize(t)` — true when `alimit` equals the real array size.
317	    /// C: `#define limitequalsasize(t) (isrealasize(t) || ispow2((t)->alimit))`
318	    #[inline]
319	    fn limit_equals_asize(&self) -> bool {
320	        self.is_real_asize() || Self::is_pow2(self.alimit)
321	    }
322	
323	    /// `ispow2realasize(t)` — is the real array size a power of two?
324	    /// C: `static int ispow2realasize (const Table *t)`.
325	    fn is_pow2_real_asize(&self) -> bool {
326	        !self.is_real_asize() || Self::is_pow2(self.alimit)
327	    }
328	
329	    /// `setlimittosize(t)` — force `alimit` to the actual real array size
330	    /// and clear the BIT_RAS flag.
331	    /// C: `static unsigned int setlimittosize (Table *t)`.
332	    fn set_limit_to_size(&mut self) -> u32 {
333	        self.alimit = self.real_asize();
334	        self.flags.set_real_asize();
335	        self.alimit
336	    }
337	
338	    // ── Hash helper functions ──────────────────────────────────────────────

local A,B = 0,{g=10}
local function f(x)
  local a = {}
  for i=1,1000 do
    local y = 0
    do
      a[i] = function () B.g = B.g+1; y = y+x; return y+A end
    end
  end
  local dummy = function () return a[A] end
  collectgarbage()
  A = 1; assert(dummy() == a[1]); A = 0;
  assert(a[1]() == x)
  assert(a[3]() == x)
  collectgarbage()
  assert(B.g == 12)
  return a
end

local a = f(10)
print("[A] after f(10)")
local x = {[1] = {}}
print("[B] before setmeta")
setmetatable(x, {__mode = 'kv'})
print("[C] after setmeta, x[1]=", x[1])
while x[1] do
  local a = A..A..A..A
  A = A+1
end
print("[D] after while, A=", A)
assert(a[1]() == 20+A); print("[E]")
assert(a[1]() == 30+A); print("[F]")
assert(a[2]() == 10+A); print("[G]")
collectgarbage(); print("[H]")
assert(a[2]() == 20+A); print("[I]")
assert(a[2]() == 30+A); print("[J]")
assert(a[3]() == 20+A); print("[K]")
assert(a[8]() == 10+A); print("[L]")
assert(getmetatable(x).__mode == 'kv'); print("[M]")
assert(B.g == 19); print("[N]")
print("done")

## Report

**Root cause:** Weak tables (`setmetatable(t, {__mode = 'kv'})`) were unimplemented — `LuaTable::set_metatable` in `crates/lua-types/src/value.rs:167` ignored the metatable's `__mode`, and `LuaTable::get`/`next_pair` never swept weakly-only-held entries. The very first thing closure.lua does after `print "testing closures"` is `local x = {[1] = {}}; setmetatable(x, {__mode = 'kv'}); while x[1] do A = A+1 end`, which is intended to spin until the GC reclaims x[1]. Without weak-table support the loop runs forever and the 30 s harness timer kills the process silently — exactly the "stops after testing closures" signature.

**Fix:** Added a `weak_mode: Cell<u8>` flag on the value.rs `LuaTable`, populated by `set_metatable` (`extract_weak_mode` scans the new metatable for a `__mode` string with `'k'`/`'v'`). `LuaTable::get` and `next_pair` now check `Rc::strong_count == 1` on weakly-tagged entries (the table itself is the only holder ⇒ unreachable except via the weak slot) and drop them, returning `Nil`.

**State after fix:** Build is green. With the weak-table fix, the script now advances through the entire first GC section, the equality block, and the closing `do` block — five sections further than before. A second, distinct hang surfaces afterward at the bytecode for `for i=1,10 do … if i==3 then break end end` (the `break` path through `OP_CLOSE`/`close_upval` when the loop body is short enough), but that is a separate codegen/upvalue bug unrelated to the weak-table root cause and is out of budget for this pass.
